questions.q: What does the abbreviation UPI stand for?
questions.explanation: UPI stands for Unified Payments Interface, India's real-time mobile payment system.

questions.q: Which organisation built and operates UPI in India?
questions.explanation: UPI was developed and is operated by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).

questions.q: In which year was UPI officially launched?
questions.explanation: UPI was launched by NPCI in 2016.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of UPI?
options: Real-time transfer of money between bank accounts via mobile
questions.explanation: UPI lets users instantly transfer money between bank accounts through a mobile phone.

questions.q: Which of these is a popular way to make a UPI payment in a shop?
questions.explanation: Scanning a QR code is one of the most common ways to pay merchants via UPI.

questions.q: A simple address like 'name@bank' used to send or receive money on UPI is called a what?
questions.explanation: A UPI ID, or Virtual Payment Address, lets users transact without sharing bank account numbers.

questions.q: Which of the following is a well-known UPI app?
questions.explanation: Google Pay is one of the major apps that operate on the UPI network in India.

questions.q: How quickly are UPI transactions typically settled between users?
options: Only at the end of the month
Only on bank working days at noon
questions.explanation: UPI transfers happen in real time, usually completing within a few seconds, around the clock.

questions.q: On how many days a week and hours a day does UPI generally operate?
options: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, including holidays
questions.explanation: UPI is designed to run 24x7, every day of the year, including weekends and holidays.

questions.q: UPI is widely cited as an example of what kind of national infrastructure?
questions.explanation: UPI is a flagship example of India's digital public infrastructure built for population scale.

questions.q: NPCI, the operator of UPI, was set up as a not-for-profit organisation under the guidance of which two bodies?
options: The Reserve Bank of India and the Indian Banks' Association
The Income Tax Department and SEBI
questions.explanation: NPCI was established by the Reserve Bank of India and the Indian Banks' Association as an umbrella organisation for retail payments.

questions.q: UPI was built as a layer on top of which earlier NPCI instant-transfer system?
questions.explanation: UPI builds upon the Immediate Payment Service (IMPS) infrastructure also created by NPCI.

questions.q: Which person, as a former chairman of UIDAI and tech adviser, is widely associated with championing India's digital public infrastructure including UPI's vision?
questions.explanation: Nandan Nilekani has been a leading advocate of India Stack and digital public infrastructure underpinning UPI.

questions.q: NPCI's own UPI app, used to send and receive money directly, is called what?
questions.explanation: BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is NPCI's official UPI app, launched in 2016.

questions.q: What does the user typically enter to authenticate and authorise a UPI payment?
options: A one-time fingerprint scan at the bank
Their full bank account password mailed by post
A physical token from the branch
A secret UPI PIN set for their account
questions.explanation: UPI transactions are authorised by entering a UPI PIN that the user sets for their linked bank account.

questions.q: Which feature lets a payee request money from a payer, who then approves it on their app?
questions.explanation: UPI supports 'collect' or request-money flows where the payer approves an incoming request.

questions.q: By the early 2020s, UPI was processing roughly what scale of transactions, making India a global leader in real-time payments?
options: Only a few lakh per decade
questions.explanation: UPI grew to process billions of transactions every month, making India the world's largest real-time payments market.

questions.q: Which broad set of open digital systems, including identity, payments and data layers, is UPI considered a key part of?
questions.explanation: UPI is a core component of 'India Stack', a set of open APIs and digital public goods.

questions.q: A major advantage of UPI's design is that it is interoperable. What does this mean?
options: It works only with one specific bank
It requires a new SIM card for each payment
Users on different apps and banks can pay each other seamlessly
questions.explanation: UPI's interoperability lets users transact across different apps and banks on a single common network.

questions.q: Which government push around 2016, involving withdrawal of high-value currency notes, is often credited with accelerating UPI adoption?
questions.explanation: The 2016 demonetisation drive spurred a shift toward digital payments, boosting UPI adoption.

questions.q: UPI typically links payments directly to what, rather than to a separate stored-value wallet?
questions.explanation: UPI moves money directly from the payer's bank account to the payee's, without needing a separate wallet.

questions.q: UPI is built on which programming-style architecture that lets third-party apps plug into the payment rails?
options: A closed proprietary system with no external access
An open API (application programming interface) architecture
questions.explanation: UPI exposes open APIs that allow banks and third-party apps to build payment services on the common rails.

questions.q: Who was serving as the founding Managing Director and CEO of NPCI during UPI's development and 2016 launch period?
questions.explanation: A. P. Hota was the founding MD and CEO of NPCI and led the organisation through UPI's launch.

questions.q: UPI allows a single mobile app to do which of the following that older systems struggled with?
options: Connect to multiple bank accounts of the user at once
questions.explanation: A single UPI app can link and operate several of a user's bank accounts together.

questions.q: Which feature, introduced to UPI, lets users approve a one-time mandate so that money is debited automatically on a future date or recurring schedule?
questions.explanation: UPI AutoPay enables recurring payments via e-mandates for subscriptions and bills.

questions.q: To use UPI internationally and link with other countries, NPCI set up a dedicated subsidiary called what?
questions.explanation: NPCI International Payments Limited (NIPL) was created to take UPI and RuPay to global markets.

questions.q: Which neighbouring country was among the first to formally adopt and link UPI for cross-border payments with India?
questions.explanation: Bhutan was among the first countries to adopt UPI, with formal linkage announced for cross-border payments.

questions.q: A notable design choice of UPI is that small person-to-person transfers generally carry what cost to the user?
options: A flat fee of several hundred rupees
Effectively free or zero merchant-style charges for users
questions.explanation: UPI person-to-person and most small payments have generally been free for users, aiding mass adoption.

questions.q: UPI's success is often contrasted with closed payment networks because it is built as which kind of good?
options: A proprietary good owned by a single foreign company
A public good / open platform serving the whole ecosystem
A luxury service for high-net-worth users only
questions.explanation: UPI is regarded as a digital public good, an open platform that any bank or app can build on.

questions.q: What is the chief reason UPI is described as having turned India into a 'world leader in digital payments'?
options: It is the only payment system on Earth
It enabled the largest volume of real-time retail transactions of any country
It replaced all of India's banks
It made cash illegal in India
questions.explanation: UPI drove India to the world's highest volume of real-time digital retail transactions, a global benchmark for such systems.
